title: Crate Table-of-Contents Production Contract

included: table-of-contents section count read (8B unsigned 64-bit integer)
section entry parsing (16B null-terminated name + 8B offset + 8B size)
standard section kind recognition (TOKENS, STRINGS, FIELDS, FIELDSETS, PATHS, SPECS)
O(1) section addressing by section kind
non-owning section byte views into the opened payload
section offset and size bounds validation against the opened payload
terminal-position invariant (TOC must end at the file's last byte)
deferred: section ownership policies (which absences are errors)

byte_layout.toc_compression: uncompressed at all accepted Crate versions. The v0.4.0 version-table note 'Compressed the Table of Contents Sections' refers to section-internal payload compression (TOKENS data, FIELDS value reps, FIELDSETS/PATHS/SPECS compressed integer arrays), not the TOC entries themselves. Reference USD writers emit TOC entries as raw bytes immediately after the bootstrap.
byte_layout.terminal_invariant: the TOC must end at the file's last byte (§16.3.8.4: 'The Table of Contents must be at the end of the file')

section_count.validation: must allow 8 + section_count * 32 bytes to fit between toc_offset and EOF, with no trailing bytes

name.trailing_bytes: bytes after the first NUL byte are undefined; readers must not extend the section name into them, must not hash the full 16 bytes as section identity, and must not compare full-16-byte raw fields for equality

offset.value: unsigned 64-bit little-endian byte offset from the start of the file

section_entry.validation: offset and offset+size must lie within the opened payload; integer overflow in the addition is an error

byte_layout.section_count_bound: no spec-mandated upper bound; implementations may reject implausibly large counts but must accept at least the six standard sections plus any vendor-defined sections present in the fixtures

laws: the table-of-contents is read as raw uncompressed bytes: 8B section count + N x 32B entries. Only section-internal payloads (TOKENS data, FIELDS value reps, compressed integer arrays in FIELDSETS/PATHS/SPECS) are LZ4-compressed; the TOC entries themselves are not
the table-of-contents must end at the file's last byte; trailing bytes after the entry list are an error
standard section kinds (TOKENS, STRINGS, FIELDS, FIELDSETS, PATHS, SPECS) must be addressable in O(1) after read_toc completes; linear name scans on every access are a contract violation
section names are 16-byte ASCII with NUL termination; bytes after the first NUL are undefined and must not influence equality, hashing, or identity
section bytes are returned as non-owning views into the opened payload; per-section copies are a contract violation
section absence is reported cleanly at the binary-format level; spec-parser policy determines which absences are errors
section offsets and sizes must lie within the opened payload; integer overflow in offset+size arithmetic is an error
this contract does not interpret section content or value representations
golden_groups: TOC round-trip on 4-section and 6-section fixtures (uncompressed entries)
missing optional sections produce absence, not error
section names with non-zero trailing bytes after the NUL are treated identical to their NUL-truncated form
duplicate section names in the TOC produce a diagnostic
section offset or size outside the payload produces a clear diagnostic
non-terminal TOC (bytes after the entry list) produces a diagnostic
O(1) section-kind lookup independent of section count
